questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The main objective of a business enterprise is to earn profit.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Profit is the driving force behind any business activity.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Profit is indeed the driving force behind business activities, and it is also the primary objective of most businesses.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The main purpose of a business is to earn profit.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Profit is not the only purpose of business as it also aims to provide employment and satisfy consumer needs.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: While profit is indeed a primary objective of most businesses, it is not the only purpose as businesses also aim to provide employment and satisfy consumer needs.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Business is a separate entity from its owner.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Business is a separate entity with its own existence and is not affected by the personal characteristics of its owner.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Businesses are indeed separate entities from their owners, with their own existence and characteristics that are not directly affected by the personal characteristics of the owner.
